perturbation.original_text: 1.1 ADVERTISING shall mean any paid advertisements, links, pointers, sponsorships, buttons, banners, navigation, or any other placements or promotions or similar services or rights on a Site, but excluding Advertising that is not paid for or which is part of an overall partnering or revenue sharing arrangement and any Product Listings.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 ADVERTISING shall mean any advertisements, links, pointers, sponsorships, buttons, banners, navigation, or any other placements or promotions or similar services or rights on a Site.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Advertising' explicitly excluded unpaid advertising and those from partnering or revenue-sharing arrangements. By removing this exclusion, the modified definition now ambiguously includes these types of advertising. This creates an inconsistency in Section 7.1.1 [ADVERTISEMENTS ON THE NEOFORMA SITE], where VerticalNet is granted the right to arrange for the sale of 'Advertising' on Neoforma Plan. If 'Advertising' now includes revenue-sharing arrangements, it's unclear whether VerticalNet's rights extend to these arrangements, potentially conflicting with other parts of the agreement dealing with revenue sharing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1.1 During the Term, VerticalNet shall have the non-exclusive right (except as to Neoforma) to arrange for the sale of Advertising on Neoforma Plan (and any other parts of the Neoforma Sites within which Neoforma elects to include Advertising) to third parties. During the Term, the parties shall meet from time to time to discuss the Advertising inventory available for sale. Any Advertising inventory that Neoforma appoints VerticalNet to arrange to sell shall not also be appointed to any third party to arrange for sale to third parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly excludes unpaid advertising and revenue-sharing arrangements from the definition of 'Advertising.' The changed text removes this exclusion, thus potentially including revenue-sharing arrangements within the definition. Section 7.1.1 grants VerticalNet the right to arrange for the sale of 'Advertising' on Neoforma Plan. If the definition now includes revenue-sharing arrangements, it becomes unclear whether VerticalNet's rights extend to these, conflicting with any other sections governing revenue-sharing. This ambiguity constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because it creates uncertainty about the scope of VerticalNet's rights and the applicability of Section 7.1.1.

perturbation.original_text: 1.12 LABORATORY PRODUCTS shall mean any equipment, instruments or other products used for scientific research and analysis in the field of human health care, including, but not limited to, the categories of equipment, instruments and products listed on EXHIBIT A, (i) that is (a) previously used and is being resold by or on behalf of the prior end-user purchaser or (b) previously sold but unused and is being resold by or on behalf of the prior end-user purchaser, and (ii) excluding Medical Products.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.12 LABORATORY PRODUCTS shall mean any equipment, instruments or other products used for scientific research and analysis in the field of human health care, including, but not limited to, the categories of equipment, instruments and products listed on EXHIBIT A.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause (i) and (ii) that defines what kind of laboratory products being sold, it creates an ambiguity to which types of products are being sold. By removing this specific clause, this can conflict with the provision set in section 3.1 and further section below where neoforma is meant to sell laboratory products to verticalnet on an exclusive basis, but the products being mentioned are the previously used and unused sold laboratory products.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Within 30 days after the Effective Date, Neoforma shall provide a copy of all Neoforma Laboratory Product Listings existing as of the Effective Date to VerticalNet for use on the VerticalNet Sites on an exclusive basis (even as to Neoforma), to the extent Neoforma has the right to do so. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the provisions of Sections 3.1 [LABORATORY PRODUCTS] through 3.8 [LABORATORY PRODUCTS] shall not apply to any Laboratory Product sold through live (non-virtual) auctions conducted by Neoforma (through Neoforma GAR or otherwise) for which no Product Listing is made; provided, however, that Neoforma shall use commercially reasonable efforts to acquire Product Listings for all such Laboratory Products. If Neoforma receives a set of Product Listings packaged as a "lot," Neoforma shall use commercially reasonable efforts to provide all Laboratory Product Listings contained in such "lot" to VerticalNet in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the definition of 'LABORATORY PRODUCTS' by removing the specific limitations related to previously used or unused products. This conflicts with section 3.1, which contemplates Neoforma providing listings of 'Neoforma Laboratory Product Listings' on an exclusive basis. By removing the limitations in the definition, the changed text introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes a 'Laboratory Product,' potentially encompassing products outside the scope intended in section 3.1, leading to a contradiction regarding the exclusivity and scope of products covered.

perturbation.original_text: 1.40 QUALIFIED LEAD shall mean a customer referred by Neoforma to VerticalNet that is not, at the time of referral, a customer of VerticalNet, which customer has agreed to place a listing on (a) the Co-Branded Career Center and/or (b) the Co-Branded Training and Education Center.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.40 QUALIFIED LEAD shall mean a customer referred by Neoforma to VerticalNet that has agreed to place a listing on (a) the Co-Branded Career Center and/or (b) the Co-Branded Training and Education Center.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'that is not, at the time of referral, a customer of VerticalNet' from the definition of 'Qualified Lead,' the changed definition now includes both existing and new customers of VerticalNet. This creates an inconsistency to section 10.6.1 where qualified ad lead which is defined, a customer referred to VerticalNet by Neoforma that is not, at the time of referral, a customer of VerticalNet.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.6.1 Except as set forth in Section 10.6.4 [ADVERTISING REVENUE], during the Term, VerticalNet shall not share any revenue derived from advertisements hosted on any VerticalNet Site with Neoforma; provided, however, that if Neoforma brings VerticalNet a Qualified Ad Lead (as defined below) for a new customer that turns into a sale of advertising on a VerticalNet Medical Online Community, VerticalNet shall pay to Neoforma a commission of [*] of the Net Advertising Revenue resulting from such sale of advertising. As used in this Section 10.6.1 [ADVERTISING REVENUE], a "Qualified Ad Lead" shall mean a customer referred to VerticalNet by Neoforma that is not, at the time of referral, a customer of VerticalNet, and which customer has agreed to place an advertisement on a VerticalNet Medical Online Community on the terms and conditions contained in VerticalNet's then current advertising policies.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'that is not, at the time of referral, a customer of VerticalNet' from the definition of 'Qualified Lead' in the changed text directly contradicts section 10.6.1, which defines 'Qualified Ad Lead' as 'a customer referred to VerticalNet by Neoforma that is not, at the time of referral, a customer of VerticalNet.' This change broadens the definition of 'Qualified Lead' to include existing VerticalNet customers, creating a direct conflict with the definition in section 10.6.1.
